The payments team owns the Stripe integration
The Stripe integration webhook secret rotates every 30 days

gold_answer: Alice (tech lead, payments team, owns Stripe integration)

pass_criteria: both expected_in_result values present in respective recall top-1

description: Long session simulation: 20 store operations then targeted recall

steps.content: The monorepo has 47 services
Services are written in Go (32), Python (8), TypeScript (7)
The oldest service is the auth service, created in 2018
The newest service is the ML inference service, created in 2025
Services communicate via gRPC internally and REST externally
Each service owns its own database schema
Cross-service data access goes through APIs, never direct DB
The platform team maintains shared libraries for auth, logging, tracing
Library versions are pinned per service, no forced upgrades
Dependency audits run weekly via Renovate
The monorepo build tool is Bazel
Build cache is shared via Remote Build Execution
Full build takes 18 minutes without cache, 3 minutes with
Services are deployed independently via their own pipelines
The critical bottleneck fact: the ML inference service deployment SLA is 45 minutes due to model loading
Service ownership is tracked in a CODEOWNERS file per service
The oncall rotation includes all senior engineers
Incident playbooks are in Notion, linked from PagerDuty
The platform SLA for shared services is 99.95% uptime
Capacity planning uses 6-month forecasts from the finance team

steps.query: Why does the ML inference service deployment take so long?

description: Consolidation preserves high-importance facts, archives noise after long time

steps.content: IMPORTANT: The master encryption key is stored in HSM, never in software
Meeting: discussed pizza for the team lunch
Meeting: someone suggested tacos instead
Meeting: decided on sushi for the team lunch
Note: the office printer was out of paper last Tuesday

steps.query: Where is the master encryption key stored?

gold_answer: HSM (Hardware Security Module), never in software
